terms.definition: A fixed-size, indexed collection storing elements of a single type (primitives or object references).
An individual value stored at a position in an array or ArrayList.
Zero-based position used to access elements in arrays and lists.
A public field on arrays that gives the number of elements.
Runtime exception thrown when accessing an invalid array index.
terms.example: int x = a[a.length]; // throws at runtime

terms.definition: Simplified loop for iterating over arrays or Iterable collections without indices.
A resizable, ordered collection backed by an array; stores object references.
Specifies the element type of a collection, providing type safety at compile time.
Automatic conversion from primitive to corresponding wrapper type.
Automatic conversion from wrapper type to primitive.
Object type that wraps a primitive (e.g., Integer for int).
A new array/list structure that reuses the same object references as the original.
terms.example: String[] b = a.clone(); // shallow copy of references

terms.definition: A copy that duplicates nested objects as well, producing fully independent structures.
terms.example: // Requires manual cloning of each element

terms.definition: Provides static methods for arrays: sort, copyOf, equals, fill, toString.
Provides algorithms for collections: sort, shuffle, reverse, frequency.
